title: Database Management Systems (DBMS)
summary: 9618 AS — DBMS software layers, data dictionary, query processor, and concurrent access control.
learningObjectives: Explain the role of the data dictionary.
Outline concurrent access and locking strategies.
Compare file-based systems with DBMS approaches.

simpleExplanation.title: Software that manages databases
simpleExplanation.summary: A DBMS provides an interface between users and stored data — handling security, integrity, backup, and efficient query execution.
steps: DDL/DML interfaces for schema and data changes.
Query processor optimises and executes SQL.
Data dictionary stores metadata (tables, fields, constraints).
Locking prevents lost updates when two users edit concurrently.

sections.content: **DBMS** (8.2) builds on **database concepts** (8.1) before **SQL** (8.3). Cambridge asks for **named DBMS functions** and **concurrency** scenarios.
